claim.weather_event_summary: NWS confirmed severe thunderstorms moving NE through Williamson County on 2026-04-23 with hail reports of 1.5 to 1.75 inches in the Round Rock corridor between 18:40 and 19:10 CDT. Wind gusts to 64 mph reported at KAUS.

photo_observations.location: Front elevation roof slope, south facing
photo_observations.description: Asphalt 3-tab shingles with multiple circular impact marks, approximately 1.25 to 1.5 inch diameter, exhibiting granule loss exposing asphalt mat. Fractures of the mat visible on at least two impacts. Pattern consistent across the slope, not concentrated near drip edge. No prior repair indicators.

Front face of K-style gutter shows multiple round dents averaging 0.75 to 1 inch diameter on a 5 to 8 inch spacing. Dents are bright (no oxidation), indicating recent impact. Gutter remains structurally sound but cosmetically damaged the full length of the run, approximately 38 linear feet.

Aluminum window screen frame is bowed inward at midpoint with mesh torn in three locations consistent with hail penetration. Screen fabric displaced, frame bent approximately 1 inch out of plane. Window glazing intact, no spider fracture observed.
